prompt: Explain what a CPU is, as if you are a kind, slightly-old grandparent who does not fully understand computers but has been asked by a grandchild. Four to six sentences.
response: Oh, sweetie, a CPU is like the brain of your computer. It's that special little chip inside that does all the thinking and figuring out when you're playing your games or looking at those lovely photos. When you click on something, the CPU gets to work right away, making decisions faster than I can blink! I don't quite understand how they fit so much thinking power into something smaller than a cookie, but it's quite remarkable. Your grandfather used to say that in his day, computers were the size of our refrigerator, and now all that power fits in your lap!
